# Summary

## What Changed

-

## Checklist

- [ ] No deprecated skill name remains.
- [ ] Markdown is multiline.
- [ ] README language links still work.
- [ ] `$game-dev-studio` is used correctly.
- [ ] References are updated if needed.
- [ ] `git diff --check` passes.
- [ ] `node scripts/validate-skill.mjs` passes.
- [ ] The skill remains engine-agnostic.
- [ ] `SKILL.md` remains short and router-like.
- [ ] Quick Check, Focused Review, Full Studio Audit, Client Studio Production Workflow, and Roadmap Strategy Audit behavior remains intact.
- [ ] Client/studio work still treats the user as final decision-maker and does not jump from vague brief to implementation.
- [ ] Client-ready visual work still requires a visual target, style bible, or approved reference before production implementation.
- [ ] Multi-phase production work still defines milestone gate evidence and client approval points.
- [ ] Implementation work still preserves repo intake, scoped slice planning, test evidence, known risks, and client decisions.
- [ ] References are lazy-loaded instead of all read by default.
- [ ] Visual tasks still prioritize image generation or complete image-generation briefs.
- [ ] UI visual design, UI motion, and gameplay VFX guidance remain intact.
- [ ] Architecture planning and giant single-file prevention remain intact.
